events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 35,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.98,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Void space observed behind trench shield panels indicating improper backfill or placement",
    "Lack of continuous soil contact against the trench shield may allow lateral movement or collapse",
    "Trench box is not providing full perimeter protection as designed"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Evacuate all personnel from the trench immediately",
    "Stop all work adjacent to the trench until conditions are resolved",
    "Have a competent person inspect and re-evaluate soil conditions and shoring design",
    "Backfill or fill voids behind trench shield panels prior to re-entry",
    "Verify the trench shield is sized correctly for depth and soil type per tabulated data"
  ],
  "rationale": "A void behind a trench shield eliminates the confinement and distribution of earth pressure that the shield requires to function. This can result in the shield shifting, tipping, or failing to protect workers from cave-ins. Per OSHA 1926 Subpart P, protective systems must be installed in accordance with manufacturer's tabulated data or registered professional engineer design, which requires competent person oversight and proper soil contact. Workers entering this trench are at imminent risk of death or serious injury; immediate evacuation and correction are mandated.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926.652(a)(1) Protection of employees in excavations",
    "OSHA 1926.652(c) Design of support systems, shield systems, and other protective systems",
    "OSHA 1926.651(k) Inspections"
  ]
}
